decisions: Lume reframed: extensible AI platform (capabilities + templates), not just an IDP. SDLC is the first/bootstrap template.
No permanent tech constraints (K8s included) — opinions become swappable options behind clean contracts. Only principles are non-negotiable.
Earned-autonomy model: high-blast/irreversible actions human-gated by default; guardrails relax as Lume proves reliability.
CLAUDE.md to be reconciled to the broader platform framing in a later follow-up (WORK-0015).
Orchestrator: general capability/template-aware core, SDLC as first instance — but 'design general, build simple' (Phase 0/1 builds simplest SDLC-only realisation; generality RESERVED).
Decomposition is a planner capability (inline for SDLC build-now; extracted when a 2nd template exists).

5 intent tools; lume_task carries lifecycle intents (RESERVED), lume_query does structured discovery; lume_manage is NOT one of the five — a potential sixth tool introduced only if lifecycle traffic demands it.
Stateless orchestrator over a swappable durable store (embedded local build-now; mechanism deferred to WORK-0005).
5-class failure model + halt-and-surface on partial failure (compensation RESERVED for destructive/invisible effects).

Agent boundaries partitioned by artifact class (domain/purpose), not create-vs-mutate: Infra owns workload manifests + deployment-composition layer (incl. CI); Provisioning owns repos/tenants/Crossplane-claims/scaffold; Obs owns observability artifacts + obs-plane workloads; Coding owns app source; Work owns work items.
Work is a deterministic service (ACT->VALIDATE only, no inference); orchestrator owns the per-workflow branch+PR and is sole writer of work-item status during execution; auto-remediation RESERVED.

Security: threat model (prompt injection incl. PLAN surface + direct intent vector, over-permissioned agent, exfiltration, operator error/approval-spoofing, semantic-malicious capability, denial-of-wallet; capability supply-chain designed-for; multi-tenant Phase 2+).
Identity: 4 static base levels + SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity with per-call mutual-TLS verification; earned autonomy = scoped/expiring/audited grants (granting is human-only high-blast).

Adopt the security primitives (SPIRE/OPA/OTel/Sigstore — pending a recommended security-stack spike), build only the AI-native defences + pipeline glue. CLAUDE.md 'no exceptions' + OSS-only reconciliation tracked in WORK-0015.

Data/state: workflow state event-sourced (model) with the simplest build-now realisation (append write-ahead records + replay-on-resume; snapshots/compaction RESERVED scale). Resume is side-effect-free; per-effect check-then-act on the one uncommitted step.

Context store layered: structured slices build-now; semantic vector store (Chroma/Qdrant candidate) + blob RESERVED/opt-in; compression local-model only; confidentiality label propagates through compression. Audit excluded from the orchestrator-callable store contract (write-only emit, separate trust domain). Local-first; networked store OSS+operator-controlled (required at multi-instance scale, not optional).

CLI is intent-first: lume "<intent>" + thin verbs (status/approve/ask/context/undo) over the 5 MCP tools; rich tree RESERVED. Self-modification is two-tier: Tier 1 (general self-improvement via the same SDLC, high-blast, no self-approval) + Tier 2 constitutional protected core (security pipeline, blast/approval logic, audit chain, grant mechanism, the core definition) NEVER grant-relaxable or autonomously self-modifiable; interim = all lume/ + core policy/config changes treated as core-touching until WORK-0014 draws the boundary.

ADR-001 (MCP two-layer): GO. Pattern proven (orchestrator is both MCP server + client of the agent, persistent session). Latency: orchestrator layer adds ~2-3ms/hop p50 over stdio (noise floor by steps=10), constant per-task, negligible vs inference. Adopt Python MCP SDK (MIT) for internal servers; TS SDK provisional for web (not exercised). PERFORMANCE GO bounded to local stdio -- networked/agents-as-pods transport unmeasured, GATED ON WORK-0008 (kagent).
notes: WORK-0007 complete: first spike. PoC in spikes/mcp-two-layer/ + ADR-001 (GO), 0 BLOCKING after 5-persona review (review targeted ADR honesty -- bounded the latency GO to local stdio, demoted TS-SDK claim, stated MIT license; the PoC code is disposable and deliberately omits the security pipeline -- don't cargo-cult). On branch spike/work-0007-mcp-two-layer; PR pending. WS-TECH workstream started. Next /iterate selects WORK-0008 (kagent eval) -- which MUST resolve the agent transport (stdio child vs networked k8s pod) and multi-agent fan-out that ADR-001's performance GO is gated on. kagent eval needs k3s in the devcontainer (docker available). OUTSTANDING FOR NEIL: (1) security-stack spike, (2) storage-stack spike, (3) protected-core-boundary governance item -- recommended, raise with WORK-0014. WORK-0015 = CLAUDE.md + cross-doc reconciliation.
